meta.name: Soft Editorial Travel UI
meta.purpose: High-level design system guidance that helps an AI replicate the look and behavior of the reference UI across an app.
meta.confidence_note: All values are inferred from the screenshot and should be treated as stylistic targets rather than exact extracted tokens.

creative_direction.style_summary: A polished, airy, premium consumer-product UI that blends Apple-like calm, travel-editorial imagery, and lightweight SaaS clarity.
creative_direction.overall_feel: The interface should feel expensive but not flashy. Favor quiet confidence, strong whitespace, soft shadows, elegant rounded corners, and concise typography over decorative effects.

design_principles: Use spacious layouts with obvious breathing room between modules. The design relies on open canvas more than dense structure.
Build the interface from white cards floating on a very pale cool background. Surfaces should feel lifted, not heavy.
Let photography carry emotion while the UI remains restrained and neutral.
Use blue as the main action and selection color, supported by slate and cool gray neutrals.
Favor rounded rectangles, pill shapes, and circular icon buttons. Hard corners break the style.
Keep contrast high for titles and primary actions, but soften supporting text with muted gray-blue tones.
Prefer one strong visual idea per component: image, status, action, or data. Avoid stacking multiple accents in the same small area.
Use borders and shadows subtly. The UI should never feel outlined, noisy, or overly neumorphic.

usage.backgrounds: Default page background should be a cool off-white, never pure gray and rarely pure white.
usage.surfaces: Primary cards and inputs sit on white surfaces with soft borders or subtle shadows.
usage.accent_strategy: Reserve saturated blue for CTAs, selected states, focused states, calendar selection, and key links.
usage.text_hierarchy: Use ink for headlines, text for body copy, and muted slate-blue for metadata, placeholders, helper text, and icons.
usage.feedback: Status colors should be softened with pale tinted backgrounds and lightly colored outlines.

font_family.primary: SF Pro Display, Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif
font_family.guidance: Use a clean neo-grotesk sans with neutral proportions and slightly friendly curves. Avoid geometric or highly branded display fonts.

display.usage: Large hero or headline moments inside major cards.

title_lg.usage: Primary card titles such as property names.

title_md.usage: Section titles, article card titles, calendar month labels.

body_lg.usage: High-visibility descriptive copy or UI labels.

body_md.usage: Default body copy, menu items, input text.

body_sm.usage: Metadata, helper text, dates, status detail.

label.usage: Small labels above controls and compact UI captions.

rules: Titles are bold and compact, with dark ink color and minimal letter-spacing.
Body text is soft and readable, never too dark unless it is interactive or essential.
Avoid all caps. Use title case or sentence case throughout.
Links should use the primary blue without becoming the loudest element in the component.

guidance: Use generous external spacing and moderate internal padding.
Tight controls can use 12-16px padding, but avoid cramped text and icon placement.
Prefer consistent optical spacing over strict mathematical compression.

radius.image: Match the card radius at exposed corners
radius.circle: Perfect circle for icon buttons, avatars, and selection controls

border.focus_treatment: Prefer a faint primary-tinted outline or shadow glow rather than a hard browser-style ring.

shadow.guidance: Shadows should be broad, diffused, and quiet. Avoid sharp drop shadows or multiple stacked shadow layers.

iconography.style: Use simple monoline icons with rounded terminals and consistent stroke weight.
iconography.color: Muted slate for default icons, primary blue for selected states, semantic colors for feedback icons.
iconography.guidance: Icons should support the layout rather than lead it. Keep them simple, centered, and slightly oversized in spacious controls.

imagery.art_direction: Use bright, atmospheric photography with cool daylight, clear subject matter, and soft contrast.
imagery.cropping: Prefer full-bleed crops with a calm focal point and enough negative space for overlays when needed.
imagery.tone: Images should feel aspirational and serene rather than gritty or oversaturated.

guidance: Use fade, lift, and small scale changes rather than dramatic movement.
Dropdowns and cards should appear softly, as if floating up into place.
Hover states should brighten or tint slightly instead of shifting aggressively.

composition.approach: Asymmetrical modular layout on a large canvas
guidance: Arrange UI in clearly separated columns or islands rather than edge-to-edge sections.
Leave substantial whitespace between major modules so each component reads like a standalone specimen.
Keep vertical rhythm calm and consistent, with repeated offsets and aligned card tops where possible.

surface_behavior.default: Most components live on white cards or inputs above the cool page background.
surface_behavior.elevation: Differentiate layers using softness, not heavy contrast.
surface_behavior.density: This is a low-density UI. Avoid compact enterprise spacing.

alignment.rule: Text and controls should align to clear internal columns, usually left-aligned.
alignment.optics: Use optical alignment with icons and circular elements so they feel centered rather than mechanically placed.

primary_button.look: Filled with primary blue, white label, medium-to-bold weight, rounded rectangle with soft shadow.
primary_button.usage: Main positive action on a card or form.

states.default: Strong blue fill with subtle depth.
states.hover: Slightly darker blue and slightly brighter shadow.
states.disabled: Desaturate into pale slate-blue with reduced contrast.

icon_circle_button.look: White circular button with soft shadow and muted slate icon.
icon_circle_button.usage: Image overlays, pagination navigation, lightweight utility actions.
icon_circle_button.rule: Keep icon centered and leave visible breathing room around it.

text_button_or_link.look: Minimal text treatment in primary blue or ink.
text_button_or_link.usage: Inline supporting action such as reviews link.
text_button_or_link.rule: Do not style inline links like heavy buttons.

text_field.look: White input with rounded corners, 1px cool border, generous horizontal padding, and muted icon support.

states.focus: Primary-tinted border or glow, not a hard outline.
states.filled: Text becomes ink while icons remain muted.

search_field.look: A standard text field with a leading search icon and optional trailing clear icon.
search_field.rule: Maintain symmetrical icon spacing and do not shrink the field below comfortable touch size.

select_field.look: Visually identical to text input until expanded.
select_field.rule: Use label, helper text, and dropdown surface to communicate selection complexity.

dropdown_menu.look: Floating white panel with rounded corners, soft shadow, and stacked options.
dropdown_menu.option_behavior: Hover and selected rows use pale tinted backgrounds, never dark highlight blocks.
dropdown_menu.separation: Use whitespace or faint dividers rather than high-contrast rules.

tag_chip.look: Small pill with pale surface tint, thin border, muted text, and compact horizontal padding.
tag_chip.rule: Tags should look gentle and secondary, not like primary CTAs.

status_pill.look: Semantic text and icon inside an outline or faintly tinted pill.
status_pill.usage: Approved, pending, rejected, or similar lightweight status labels.
status_pill.rule: Use color economically. The pill background should be very light.

radio.look: Large circular control with thin cool-gray stroke and generous inner spacing.
radio.selected_state: Fill or dot should be precise and centered, using primary blue.

checkbox.look: Rounded square with primary blue fill when selected and white checkmark.
checkbox.rule: Unchecked state should stay light and outlined.

switch.look: Slim rounded track with circular thumb and cool-gray outline.
switch.off_state: White or tinted track with muted border.
switch.on_state: Primary-led track or primary-accent thumb depending on system choice.
switch.rule: Keep the switch visually calm; do not make it glossy or oversized.

avatar.look: Circular photo crop, slightly larger than typical dense UI avatars.
avatar.rule: Use a white ring when avatars overlap or sit on image-heavy backgrounds.

avatar_group.look: Horizontally overlapping avatar stack with 10-14px overlap.
avatar_group.rule: Keep overlap tight enough to feel connected but loose enough that each portrait remains identifiable.

hero_property_card.look: Large editorial card with full-bleed image top, bold title, metadata, rating row, amenity icons, description, and pricing footer.
hero_property_card.surface: White card with soft elevation and large radius.
hero_property_card.image_treatment: Wide scenic image with matching rounded top corners and overlay controls.
content_structure: Image zone with circular back button and small pagination chip.
Rating summary with stars and review link.
Amenity row with line icons and text labels.
Body copy with comfortable line spacing.
Footer with primary CTA on the left and bold price on the right.
hero_property_card.rule: This card is the showcase element. Let it be visually dominant and more spacious than standard cards.

editorial_article_card.look: Medium-sized card with image header, bold headline, short teaser, and author row.
editorial_article_card.surface: White, soft shadow, 24px padding in text area.
editorial_article_card.tone: Feels like a premium magazine tile, not a corporate dashboard widget.

utility_menu_card.look: Vertical list card with icon-led rows, faint row separators or alternating hover treatment, and rounded container.
utility_menu_card.rows: Profile, settings, integrations, theme toggle, sign out, or similar utilities.
utility_menu_card.rule: Rows should be generous, calm, and clearly tappable.

calendar_card.look: Large white card with centered month heading, arrow controls, airy date grid, and one strong selected day.
calendar_card.rule: The calendar must feel light and breathable. Empty space is part of the design.

feedback_banner.look: Horizontal alert card with very light semantic tint, subtle border, left accent line, leading icon, and trailing dismiss action.
feedback_banner.rule: Keep feedback surfaces reassuring and soft rather than urgent unless the message is truly critical.

list_item.look: Single-line or two-line row with icon left, label center, optional badge or control right.
list_item.interaction: Use pale hover tint and smooth background fade.

badge.look: Small circular red count badge with white text.
badge.rule: Use sparingly as a point accent inside otherwise neutral components.

inline_alert.look: A soft semantic banner with icon, bold short title, optional supporting text, and dismiss icon.
inline_alert.hierarchy: The title should be slightly darker or bolder than the supporting line.
inline_alert.semantic_style: Success, warning, and danger should all use pale tinted backgrounds and restrained outlines.

toast_or_notice.look: If used elsewhere, follow the same soft semantic treatment as inline alerts, with rounded corners and minimal noise.

stars.look: Warm yellow stars paired with dark numeric rating and blue review link.
stars.rule: The stars are cheerful but still restrained. Do not make them overly glossy or cartoonish.

header.look: Centered bold month label with left and right chevrons placed near the card edges.
header.rule: Use generous top padding and preserve wide spacing around controls.

weekday_row.look: Muted small labels with even horizontal spacing.

date_cell.look: Simple centered number inside an invisible cell until active.
date_cell.selected_state: Filled primary rounded square or soft rounded tile with white text.
date_cell.current_day_hint: Use a small blue dot or subtle cue instead of a heavy ring.

system_rules_for_unseen_components.tables: If the app needs tables, render them as card-contained, low-density data views with generous row height, soft dividers, and strong typographic hierarchy rather than grid-heavy enterprise tables.
system_rules_for_unseen_components.modals: Use large-radius white panels on dimmed cool overlays. Keep headers minimal and actions obvious.
system_rules_for_unseen_components.tabs: Prefer understated segmented controls or pill tabs with primary selection and pale inactive surfaces.
system_rules_for_unseen_components.navigation: Use icon-led, low-noise navigation with clear active states in blue and a lot of whitespace.
system_rules_for_unseen_components.charts: Use calm, minimal charts with blue-led accents, light grid lines, and rounded tooltip surfaces.

do_not_do: Do not use dark mode aesthetics, glassmorphism, or neon accents as defaults for this system.
Do not compress spacing to fit more content. The visual identity depends on roominess.
Do not use harsh black text, heavy borders, or sharp corners.
Do not mix multiple saturated accent colors into a single component without a semantic reason.
Do not make cards feel flat and border-boxed; they should feel softly elevated.
Do not over-animate. Motion should support polish, not announce itself.

ai_replication_instructions.core_prompt: Design the interface as a premium light-theme system made of rounded white cards on a cool off-white canvas, using bold dark navy headlines, muted slate secondary text, soft blue primary actions, airy spacing, subtle borders, diffused shadows, and serene editorial photography.
ai_replication_instructions.component_prompt: When inventing new components, keep the same low-density spacing, 12-28px radii, soft blue-gray palette, monoline icons, gentle semantic tints, and card-first composition. New elements should feel like they belong next to a modern travel, lifestyle, or premium SaaS UI.
ai_replication_instructions.layout_prompt: Favor asymmetrical modular compositions with large gaps between islands of content. Each major element should feel individually framed and easy to scan.
